footer.tagline: Managed performance engineering, at startup speed.

hero.headline:
Your systems are slow.
Your team doesn't know why.
hero.subheadline: Vantix is your external performance and SRE team. Monitoring, load testing, and observability as a continuously improving managed capability.

pain.heading: Does this sound familiar?
pain.subheading: If you're a CTO or engineering manager at a scaling SaaS, you've probably faced at least one of these.

cards.title: Alerts firing at 3 AM
cards.body: Your on-call rotation is exhausted and alerts are noise — no one knows which ones actually matter.

cards.title: Monitoring tools, zero insights
cards.body: You have Datadog or Grafana, but no one has time to build the dashboards that tell you something useful.

cards.title: Load tests that don't exist
cards.body: You find out about performance bottlenecks in production, from customers, at the worst possible moment.

cards.title: No SRE, no budget for one
cards.body: A senior SRE costs $150K+/yr. You need the expertise without the full-time hire.

cards.title: Every release is a guess
cards.body: Deploys go out without any baseline — you don't know if today's release made things better or worse.

cards.title: Incident retrospectives go nowhere
cards.body: You write up the post-mortem. Nothing changes. The same incident happens again three months later.

services.heading: What we deliver
services.subheading: Three managed capabilities, designed to be combined.

observability.headline: See everything that matters.
observability.description: We instrument your systems, build the dashboards that actually answer your questions, and tune your alerts so they fire when something is wrong — not just when a threshold is crossed.
features: Curated dashboards by service and team
Alert tuning — noise reduction, actionable runbooks

performance.headline: Know your limits before your users do.
performance.description: Continuous k6 load testing built into your CI/CD pipeline. We write the scripts, baseline your services, define the SLOs, and track them over time.
features: k6 load test suite (peak, soak, spike)

sre.headline: Senior SRE expertise, startup budget.
sre.description: A dedicated SRE (fractional) embedded in your engineering team. Architecture review, reliability roadmap, incident command, and the hands-on work to make it real.

pricing.heading: Transparent pricing
pricing.subheading: No surprise invoices. No "contact us for pricing." Three tiers — pick what fits.

starter.name: Starter
starter.description: One managed capability to get started.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.description: All three services plus fractional SRE.

cta_section.heading: Ready to stop guessing?
cta_section.subheading: Book a 30-minute strategy call. We'll review your current setup and show you exactly what we'd change first.

obs_page.meta_description: Managed observability: instrumentation, dashboards, SLOs, and alert tuning as a continuously improving capability. From chaotic to reliable.

hero.headline: Your team doesn't lack observability.
hero.headline_accent: They lack a way to execute it well.
hero.subheadline: Alerts being ignored. Dashboards that don't answer the questions you have during an incident. Telemetry bill growing out of control. That's not solved with more tools. It's solved with someone who operates them correctly.

problem.body_1: If your monitoring stack feels 'implemented' but chaotic, it's probably the same story: alerts no one trusts, so they get ignored. Dashboards that don't answer the questions you have during an incident. Telemetry volumes growing — and with them, the bill. Inconsistent instrumentation across teams. And everyone assuming 'the platform team will fix it'... between emergencies.
problem.body_2: That's exactly what we solve. This is not a tool. This is not a one-off project. It's a managed capability — designed to keep your signals useful, your costs controlled, and your team confident when something breaks.

deliverables.heading: What our clients receive
deliverables.intro: Six concrete deliverables that transform your monitoring from 'implemented but chaotic' to 'reliable and useful'.

items.title: An observability baseline — what to collect, what to discard, and why
items.body: We audit your current stack and define an opinionated telemetry strategy: which metrics matter, which logs are noise, which traces you need, and what data you're paying to collect that no one ever looks at. We instrument with OpenTelemetry up to 5 services.

items.title: Practical SLOs and alerts — aligned to how the business experiences downtime
items.body: We don't define generic '99.9% uptime' SLOs. We define SLOs the business understands: 'if checkout p95 exceeds 2 seconds, we're losing sales.' Error budget tracking with burn rate alerting so the team knows when to prioritize reliability over features.

items.title: Lean, consistent, opinionated dashboards — built around the questions your team actually asks
items.body: Not 40 dashboards where no one finds anything. 10-15 Grafana dashboards designed to answer specific incident questions: which service is failing? Since when? What changed? What's the impact? RED metrics, USE metrics, PostgreSQL, and SLO compliance — each with a clear purpose.

items.title: Continuous instrumentation improvement — not quarterly fire drills
items.body: Every month we review alerts (false positives, gaps, thresholds), onboard new services, adjust dashboards to team feedback, and update SLOs if the business changed. It's iterative improvement, not a project that ends and gets forgotten.

items.title: Optimized telemetry and controlled costs — cardinality and volume reduction
items.body: If your Datadog or New Relic bill grows every month and no one knows why, this is for you. We identify high-cardinality metrics, verbose logs no one reads, and redundant traces. We reduce volume without losing visibility. Some clients reduce their telemetry bill 30-50% with this optimization alone.

items.title: Async-first communication — with calls when they really matter
items.body: Slack for day-to-day questions. Loom for explaining findings. PDF for formal reports. We include a 15-minute kick-off call to align, optional calls for incidents or findings, and an optional video walkthrough of the monthly report if you want to present results to your team or management.

items.title: Fewer surprises
items.body: Alerts people trust. Incidents detected before users complain. Error budget as a prioritization tool.

items.title: Faster diagnosis
items.body: Dashboards that answer incident questions in 30 seconds, not 30 minutes. End-to-end correlation with traces.

items.title: A platform that improves every month
items.body: This is not a project that gets delivered and forgotten. It's a capability that's continuously refined. Every month more precise, more useful, more efficient.

steps.title: Kick-off
steps.body: We understand your current stack, your pain points, your tools. If you prefer async, send a doc and we start.

steps.title: Weeks 1-3: Baseline
steps.body: Audit + OTel instrumentation + dashboards + alerts. By the end of week 3, you have a functional, opinionated observability stack.

steps.title: Weeks 3-4: SLOs + Governance
steps.body: Formal SLOs, error budget tracking, burn rate alerting. Your team knows when to prioritize reliability.

steps.title: Month 2+: Continuous management
steps.body: Monthly alert review, service onboarding, cost optimization, instrumentation improvement.

steps.title: Every quarter: Workshop
steps.body: Training for your team on using dashboards, interpreting SLOs, and diagnosing incidents.

steps.title: Optional: Monthly video meet
steps.body: Walkthrough of observability status, improvement metrics, and next steps.

sre_page.meta_description: A dedicated fractional SRE embedded in your team. Architecture review, incident command, reliability roadmap — senior SRE expertise at startup budget.

hero.headline: Senior SRE expertise.
hero.subheadline: A dedicated SRE embedded in your engineering team — handling architecture review, incident command, post-mortems, and the hands-on reliability work that never gets prioritized. All the expertise of a full-time senior SRE at a fraction of the cost.

problem.body_1: A senior SRE costs $150K+/year in salary alone. Your engineering team is shipping features, not building reliability practices. Incidents take hours to resolve because no one owns the process. Post-mortems get written and filed — then the same incident happens again three months later. You know reliability matters, but you can't justify a full-time hire at this stage.
problem.body_2: That's the exact gap a fractional SRE fills. Not a consultant who delivers a report and disappears — a dedicated practitioner embedded in your team, owning reliability the same way your engineers own their services.

deliverables.heading: What our clients receive
deliverables.intro: Six concrete deliverables that bring senior SRE capability to your team without the full-time headcount.

items.title: Dedicated fractional SRE embedded in your team (10-20 hrs/week)
items.body: Your fractional SRE attends standups, reviews PRs for reliability impact, participates in architecture discussions, and is reachable on Slack during business hours. Not a consultant who shows up for quarterly reviews — a practitioner who knows your systems.

items.title: Architecture review + reliability roadmap
items.body: In the first two weeks, we map your architecture, identify the top reliability risks, and produce a prioritized roadmap. Not a generic checklist — a concrete plan specific to your stack: which services are single points of failure, which have no SLOs, which incidents are preventable.

items.title: Incident command and post-mortem facilitation
items.body: During live incidents, your fractional SRE leads the response: coordinates communication, drives the diagnosis process, and keeps the team focused. After the incident, facilitates a structured post-mortem that produces actionable follow-ups — not a document that gets filed and forgotten.

items.title: Escalation path during live incidents
items.body: When something breaks at 2 AM, your team has a direct escalation path. We respond within 15 minutes for P1 incidents during business hours, and maintain an async escalation channel for off-hours emergencies. Your on-call rotation gets backed by someone who has seen the failure mode before.

items.title: SLO program definition and tracking
items.body: We define SLOs your business understands, instrument them correctly, and establish the error budget process. Monthly SLO reviews with the engineering team — is reliability improving? Are we spending error budget on the right things? This becomes the language between engineering and product.

items.title: Async communication (Slack) + optional weekly sync
items.body: All communication defaults to async: Slack for questions, Loom for explaining architectural decisions, written reports for status updates. An optional 30-minute weekly sync to align on priorities. We respect your team's time — meetings happen when they add value that async doesn't.

items.title: Reliability without headcount
items.body: Senior SRE capability embedded in your team at a fraction of the cost. No recruiting, no onboarding overhead, no $150K salary commitment.

items.title: Faster incident resolution
items.body: Clear incident command process. Runbooks that exist. A practitioner who knows your systems and leads the response. Incidents that used to take 4 hours get resolved in 45 minutes.

items.title: A reliability culture that sticks
items.body: SLOs your team actually tracks. Post-mortems that produce changes. Architecture decisions made with reliability data. The practices don't disappear when the engagement ends — they're built into how your team works.

steps.title: Kick-off
steps.body: We understand your current architecture, recent incidents, and what reliability means for your business. Access to your stack is set up — Slack, incident tooling, monitoring.

steps.title: Week 1: Architecture review
steps.body: We map your services, identify single points of failure, review recent incidents, and audit your current on-call setup. You get a written risk assessment by end of week 1.

steps.title: Weeks 2-3: Reliability roadmap
steps.body: We build the prioritized roadmap: what to fix first, what SLOs to define, what runbooks to write. The first quick wins get implemented — things that improve reliability immediately.

steps.title: Month 2+: Embedded SRE
steps.body: Ongoing embedded work: incident response, PR reviews, SLO tracking, architecture guidance. Your team has a dedicated reliability practitioner in their corner.

steps.title: Quarterly: Reliability review
steps.body: A structured review of reliability progress: SLO trends, incident frequency, error budget consumption, and roadmap for the next quarter.

mission_page.meta_description: Vantix's mission: every tech team knows exactly how their system behaves under load — and has a concrete plan to improve it.

mission.label: Mission
mission.heading: That every technology team knows exactly how their system behaves under load — and has a concrete plan to improve it.
mission.body: We close the gap between 'having tools' and 'using them well'. We make monitoring, testing, and observability work continuously and reliably — as a managed capability that improves every month, not as a project that ends and gets forgotten.

vision.label: Vision
vision.heading: To be the external Performance and SRE team that tech companies across the Americas turn to when they need their system to be reliable — without building an internal team of 10 people to achieve it.
vision.body: In 3 years, every tech company in LATAM and the US that needs performance engineering, observability, or SRE practices should think of Vantix as the first option: faster than hiring, deeper than a tool, and more reliable than doing it alone.

purpose.label: Purpose
purpose.heading: That no team has to cross their fingers before a Black Friday, a launch, or a marketing campaign. That infrastructure decisions are made with data, not hope.

items.title: Data, not opinions
items.body: Every number in every report comes from real tests and monitoring running 24/7. We don't say 'I think'. We say 'the data shows'. If we don't have the data, we measure before we opine.

items.title: Actions, not lists
items.body: We don't deliver generic best-practice lists. We deliver the exact SQL, the exact config, the exact change — with the expected improvement estimate. 'CREATE INDEX idx_x ON table(col1, col2)' not 'consider optimizing the database'.

items.title: Async by default
items.body: We respect our clients' time and our own. Slack for communication, Loom for explanations, PDF for reports. Meetings exist when they truly matter — not as ritual.

items.title: No lock-in
items.body: Everything we build uses open-source tools: k6, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus. If you decide to end the service tomorrow, everything is yours. We don't create dependency — we create capability.

items.title: Knowledge transfer
items.body: Our goal is for your team to learn. Workshops, pair programming, code reviews, mentoring. A successful Vantix engagement ends when your team no longer needs us — and that is our best success story.

items.title: Continuous improvement, not projects
items.body: We don't deliver a report that gets filed away. We deliver a cycle: measure → find → prioritize → implement → validate. Every month the baseline rises. Every month the system is more reliable. That's what we sell.

manifesto.label: What we believe
items: Most teams don't lack tools. They lack someone who operates them correctly.
A load test run once is not performance engineering. It's a checkbox.
Alerts that get ignored are worse than no alerts — they create a false sense of security.
The cost of an incident is always greater than the cost of preventing it.
A dashboard that doesn't answer the question you have during an incident is decoration, not observability.
The difference between a tester and a performance engineer is the difference between running a script and understanding the architecture.
100% async doesn't mean 0% communication. It means efficient communication, when it matters, without wasting anyone's time.
The best deliverable is the one that arrives without you asking for it, with the data you need, in the format you can use.
